description.content: Single multiplexed endpoint used by Steps 1, 3, 9, 11-16, 19-20, 22-23, 25, 28, 30-31, 33, 37-38. The `ProcessName` field distinguishes which check is performed; `Segment` is required for segment-scoped checks and omitted for global ones. V5: `TradeDate` (YYYY-MM-DD) is now required on every call to this endpoint.

response.name: Status check result. MSG=SKIP (holiday check) or TRUE/True means proceed; FALSE/False means not yet ready.

item.name: Step 39: Get Expected Filename by Segment and Upload ID

request.name: Step 39: Get Expected Filename by Segment and Upload ID

description.content: Returns the expected file name pattern for a given segment and Upload ID. Use this to verify your file is correctly named before upload. The pattern includes the embedded trade date in DDMMYY format.

item.name: Create or trigger a Trade Process (Steps 2, 10, 34)

request.name: Create or trigger a Trade Process (Steps 2, 10, 34)

description.content: PROCESSID='0' creates a new Process ID and returns the 28-step pipeline (Step 2), or for GROUPNAME='FOPositionChange' creates the Corporate Action Position Change process (Step 34 Phase 1). Passing the real (non-zero) PROCESSID triggers sequential execution of all pipeline steps (Step 10, or Step 34 Phase 2).

response.name: Process ID and pipeline step list/status

description.content: Retrieve file format requirements (name pattern, extension, column count) before uploading, for a given Upload ID.

response.name: File format requirements

description.content: Upload the file in chunks. For files > 1 MB, call this multiple times incrementing CurrentChunk from 0 to TotalChunks-1. A new GUID must be generated per upload.

(Required) New GUID per upload
(Required)

response.name: Chunk upload acknowledgement. Save the returned Guid — it is used as uploadfoldername in Step 7.

description.content: (Required)

item.name: Step 7: File Details Entry in CBOS Source

request.name: Step 7: File Details Entry in CBOS Source

description.content: Register the uploaded file in CBOS so the backend EXE picks it up from the GUID folder. Must be called once per file. The GUID in uploadfoldername must exactly match the GUID returned in Step 5.

response.name: File entry registered for backend EXE pickup

item.name: Step 8: Skip Optional Subprocess in Template

request.name: Step 8: Skip Optional Subprocess in Template

description.content: Used when an optional file upload step is not required for a given trade date. Marks the step as skippable before triggering the process. Applies to steps where ISOPTIONALVISIBLE=true in the Step 2 response.

BUTTONNAME=REFRESH polls whether a valuation run is in progress (Step 17; empty Table1 = not yet triggered). BUTTONNAME=COLLATERAL_VALUATION_DATEWISE triggers date-wise Collateral Valuation (Step 18). This starts the process asynchronously — re-check the GTG endpoint until MSG=TRUE.

response.name: Table1 empty = not yet triggered; populated = process started/running

description.content: Triggers MTF Collateral Allocation. Re-poll the Collateral Allocation GTG (Step 19) to confirm completion before proceeding to Fund Transfer.
Triggers MTF Fund Transfer. Requires the SQL Server Agent job 'MTF_RISK_UPDATE' to exist on the target server — otherwise returns an error indicating the job does not exist.

response.name: Fund transfer result (or SQL job-not-found error)

description.content: Runs a specific MTF sub-process for the given date, selected via the TYPE field: BUY PROCESS (Step 26), BUY POSTING (Step 27), SELL PROCESS AND POSTING (Step 29), WEEKLY AUTOCLOSURE (Step 32, run only at 4 PM on trading days). MTF processes depend on EQ Bill Posting having completed (see Step 25 GTG).

item.name: Daily Margin Reporting - Check Status / Trigger (Steps 35-36)

request.name: Daily Margin Reporting - Check Status / Trigger (Steps 35-36)

description.content: BUTTONNAME=REFRESH polls the combined margin engine (Step 35; empty Table1 = not triggered yet). BUTTONNAME=COMBINEDMARGIN_PROCESS triggers the combined daily margin reporting process for the given date (Step 36).

response.name: Table1 empty = not yet triggered; populated = process started/running

description.content: Look up the Process ID for the current segment and trade date to confirm what was created in Step 2.

API Reference & Integration Guide for the EDP Trade Process — a 39-step workflow (Steps 1-39) covering Segment selection, Process ID creation, file upload, process triggering, post-process status checks, collateral & fund transfer, MTF process suite, corporate actions, and margin reporting. The same 39-step workflow applies to all 10 supported market segments (EQ, DR, CUR, SLB, NCDEX, NCDEXPHY, MCX, MCXPHY, NSECOM, MF) — only the Segment code and relevant file names change.

Step type legend: GTG = Good to Go, CHK = Check Status, TRG = Trigger, MTF = MTF Process, CORE = Core API.

V5 change: a `TradeDate` field (format YYYY-MM-DD) was added to every `file_process_status` call (Steps 1, 3, 9, 11-16, 19-20, 22-23, 25, 28, 30-31, 33, 37-38). It appears immediately after `Segment` where present, or as the first field where `Segment` is absent. All other steps (2, 4-8, 10, 17-18, 21, 24, 26-27, 29, 32, 34-36, 39) are unchanged from V4.
